audience: General public; all ages; multi-day regional fair with livestock
contact_name: Vendor/exhibitor opportunities expected at this large regional fair; contact fair directly for application details.
column23: Full fair runs June 23–27; window-relevant dates June 25–27. Theme: Stars & Stripes and Summer Nights honoring the nation's 250th anniversary. Two entertainment stages. Facebook: facebook.com/MeramecCommunityFair

audience: General public all ages; community fair with livestock shows, carnival rides, rodeo, concerts, food vendors
tabling_details: Large public county fair; commercial vendors historically present; contact fairgrounds directly for vendor booth applications
column21: Fair runs June 24–27; currently underway as of run date with 2+ days remaining. Admission $15–$20 adults, $5–$10 ages 7–12, under 7 free.

venue: Crites Park / Old State Hwy A
audience: General public; all ages; ~18,000+ attendees; includes Seniors' Fishing Derby June 27
tabling_details: Vendor application via Festus City Hall or online at secure.rec1.com/MO/festus-mo/catalog (under Firecracker Festival)
notes: 37th annual festival. Fri June 26 3pm–11pm and Sat June 27. Carnival, food, fireworks. Seniors' Fishing Derby at Al Brown Lake in Crites Park on June 27 10am–12pm with prizes.

audience: General public; family-friendly county fair with parade, livestock shows, demolition derby, truck/tractor pulls, 4-H and FFA exhibits, live music, midway
tabling_details: Vendor booths available; vendors receive 2 Pit Passes (extra must be purchased). Contact fair board for 2026 booth costs and registration.
column21: Held second full weekend in July each year; 2026 falls July 10–12. Vendor page: stegenevievecountyfair.com/vendors.

audience: General public all ages; 5-day county fair with 4-H/FFA exhibits, concerts, demo derby, horse shows, truck and tractor pulls
tabling_details: Fair historically accepts commercial/food vendors; contact lincolncountymofair@gmail.com for 2026 vendor booth applications and pricing
column22: 65th anniversary of Lincoln County Fair. Check Facebook page 'Lincoln County Fair-MO' for 2026 vendor updates.

audience: General public county fair; all ages; large annual event drawing thousands
tabling_details: Vendor applications accepted through the fairgrounds. Contact fair office for booth types, costs, and availability.
column21: Commercial and exhibitor vendor applications accepted through jeffersoncountyfair.net.

audience: General public all ages; 6-day county fair with rodeo, livestock, entertainment, vendors; dedicated Senior Citizens Day for residents 65+
tabling_details: Fair actively invites retail vendors and exhibitors; register via https://mcfa.org/become-an-exhibitor/
column21: Theme: Stars, Stripes & Summer Nights. July 20–25. Senior Citizens Day est. Wed July 22; 65+ residents with coffee & donuts, music, bingo, King & Queen contest; capped at 1,400 seniors. $5 admission for 65+ until noon on Senior Day.

event_name: Montgomery County Fair – Senior Citizens Day
audience: Montgomery County residents 65+; senior living facilities and senior citizen groups; capped at 1,400 attendees
tabling_details: Fair vendor/exhibitor booths available throughout fair week; Senior Citizens Day draws the largest 65+ audience — prime day for a Medicare information table. Contact fair for booth availability on this specific day.
column21: Wednesday of fair week; estimated July 22 2026. Activities: coffee & donuts, entertainment, King & Queen contest for senior groups, lunch, bingo, door prizes. 65 & Older Show check-in 8:30–10:00 AM.

audience: General public all ages; large family and senior attendance from across St. Charles County
tabling_details: Exhibitor/vendor booths available; contact fair organizers at stcharlescofair.org for cost and application
column21: Annual 5-day county fair held 4th week of July. 4-H demos, livestock shows, carnival rides, entertainment, commercial exhibits. High foot traffic; strong senior presence across St. Charles County.

audience: General public; county fair with agricultural exhibits, livestock, competitions, swap meet, entertainment
tabling_details: Vendors welcome; swap meet and commercial vendor opportunities. Mailing address: P.O. Box 25, Bismarck MO 63624. Contact fair for 2026 booth costs.
column21: 152nd annual fair. 3-day event July 30–August 1 2026. FairEntry portal: fairentry.com/Public/EnterFair/24037

audience: General public county fair; all ages; senior (60+) admission discount available at gate
tabling_details: Vendors required to participate all five days. Senior (60+)/Military discount at gate Thu–Sat. Contact fair for vendor application and booth costs.
column21: August 5–9 2026. Large annual fair in Franklin County seat. Concerts (Tucker Wetmore Aug 6), livestock shows, exhibits, midway rides, motorsports. Senior age 60+ discounted admission at gate.

event_name: Jour de Fete Arts and Crafts Festival
audience: General public; southeast Missouri's largest outdoor crafts fair attracting tens of thousands; all ages
tabling_details: 150–200+ vendor/craft booths. Vendor applications accepted via official website. Contact organizers for 2026 booth costs and deadlines.
column21: Held second full weekend in August annually. Large public event; primarily arts/crafts vendors but information table likely possible given scale. Also at visitstegen.com/jour-de-fete/

audience: General public; family-friendly county fair with livestock, exhibits, 4-H competitions, entertainment
tabling_details: Fair accepts exhibitors and vendors; official entry forms available. Exhibit pickup Sunday August 23 10:00 AM–1:00 PM. Contact fairgrounds for commercial vendor/booth details.
column21: FairEntry.com lists 2026 Perry County Community Fair August 18–22. See also perrycountyfairgrounds.com/

audience: General public; strong senior demographic; one of St. Louis region's largest street festivals with 300+ vendors
tabling_details: 300+ vendor booths (arts, crafts, antiques, food); contact festival organizers at festivalofthelittlehills.com for vendor application and booth cost
column21: Fri Aug 21 4pm–10pm; Sat Aug 22 9am–10pm; Sun Aug 23 9am–4pm. Free admission. 300+ arts/crafts/antique vendors plus 50 food booths and live music.

audience: General public community festival; all ages; dedicated Seniors in the Park concert and lunch program
tabling_details: Craft fair Saturday Sept 12 9am–3pm; 10×10 booth $50 early bird (before May 31). Note: craft fair is for physical items only. Contact chamber for sponsorship/informational booth options.
column21: 31st annual event hosted by Twin City Area Chamber of Commerce. Golf tournament, car & tractor show, dog show, children's activities, craft fair, and Seniors in the Park concert and lunch. Chamber office Tue/Wed/Thu 10am–2pm.

audience: General public; county fair with agricultural exhibits, livestock, entertainment, competitions
tabling_details: County fair; vendor/exhibitor opportunities typically available. Contact fair directly for 2026 commercial booth details.

audience: General public and families; large annual community festival drawing thousands; senior-friendly with explicit nonprofit info booth category
tabling_details: Nonprofit information booths, artisan booths, and commercial booths available. Formal application deadline was Jan 30 2026 — contact organizers for late/waitlist availability.
notes: Annual festival in Kirkwood Park. Food, live music, arts & crafts, car show, sailboat regatta, parade, folklife artisans, dog show. Nonprofit info booth application deadline was Jan 30 — contact adamssa@kirkwoodmo.org / 314-822-5855 for late/waitlist spots.

audience: General public community festival; all ages; city of Arnold's annual incorporation celebration since 1973
tabling_details: Informational Booth is a specific vendor category offered by the City of Arnold at arnoldmo.org. Also craft, food, and parade vendor categories. Contact city for 2026 application and fees.
column21: Starts Friday Sept 19 5pm through Sunday Sept 21. Informational booths, craft booths, food vendors, carnival rides, concerts, car show, fishing derby, Sunday parade. City offers a dedicated Informational Booth application — strong fit for Medicare broker table.

audience: General public all ages; large annual community street festival with vendors, food trucks, live music, lawn games
tabling_details: Vendor spaces available via online application or PDF packet; first-come-first-served. Mail payment to: Warrenton Fall Festival, 200 West Booneslick, Warrenton MO 63383; or email Jon Struckhoff with subject 'Fall Festival 2026'.
column21: September 19 2026 12:00 PM–9:30 PM on Main Street. Headliner: The Marshall Tucker Band 7:30 PM. Lawn Game Village on N East Street.
